prompts: A liquid crystal display (LCD) operates by manipulating light using liquid crystals, which can change their orientation when an electric current is applied. The display consists of two polarized glass panels with liquid crystal solution between them. When no current is applied, the liquid crystals allow light to pass through only one polarizer, appearing opaque. When current is applied, the crystals twist, letting light pass through both polarizers. Backlighting from LED sources illuminates the display, and color filters create images by controlling the intensity and color of light in each pixel via red, green, and blue sub-pixels.
